summary: R2 systems re-review accepts the schema/error-model fix. validateWorldStateV0 now guards the M3 arrays and entry shapes introduced by the succession task before runtime reference and hash validation, including M3CharacterStateV0.incapacitated, succession candidate profiles, support sources, crises, triggers, candidates, and outcomes. The previous raw values-is-not-iterable failure is resolved: independent malformed-state probes now return structured invalid-schema paths without throwing. The implementation remains within M3 minimal succession scope and is ready for QA review.

tests_run.result: Worktree contains task-scoped protocol/sim-core/test edits and project state/message files for M3-SUCCESSION-STATE-MACHINE-001. No forbidden app, UI, content, save-format, gate/status/checksum/report paths appear.

Tracked diffs are packages/protocol/src/index.ts, packages/sim-core/package.json, packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts, packages/sim-core/src/world-state-v0.ts, project goal/model/task/thread state, and touched tests. These are within the task allowlist.
Untracked files are task message/route handoffs and tests/sim-core-m3-succession-state-machine.test.ts; all are within allowed project/messages or tests scope.

decisions: Accept the R2 schema/error-model fix and route to qa_reviewer, per the routed handoff request and R3 review policy.
The previous systems_architect blocker is resolved: malformed M3 succession state is now rejected as structured invalid-schema before canonical hash formatting can throw.

The R2 patch stays scoped to sim-core state validation and focused tests; it does not add UI, save-format support, content schema/runtime changes, full civil war, full diplomacy, formal dynasty rules, or M4+ war behavior.
The original architectural conclusions still hold: command authority remains through shared GameCommand paths, candidate support is explicit/serializable, and deterministic tie-breakers are stable.
risks: QA still needs an independent read-only pass for the full R3 task, including protocol/state schema, command authority, deterministic transitions, dead/incapacitated invariants, office vacancy handling, and regression evidence.
Succession thresholds and support weights remain M3 scaffold constants, not final dynasty balance or historical succession rules.
requested_action: Perform independent QA review for M3-SUCCESSION-STATE-MACHINE-001, including the R2 malformed-state schema/error-model fix, full required validation evidence, and R3 protocol/state/authority/determinism coverage before lead acceptance.
